mnemonics: DICE: Default Interface methods Can Evolve
OLD IS GOLD: Interfaces Support Growth with Old Logic Defaults
keyPoints: Default methods allow adding new functionality to interfaces without breaking existing implementations.
A class implementing multiple interfaces with conflicting default methods must override the method to resolve the conflict.
Default methods are public by default and do not need to be declared with the 'public' modifier (though it's allowed).
commonMistakes: Assuming a class inherits default methods from its superclass if the interface is the same (it inherits from the interface, not the class hierarchy).
Forgetting to override the method when two implemented interfaces have a default method with the same signature, resulting in a compilation error.
